data.title: CARsgen Therapeutics' Substantial Shareholder Boosts Shareholding
Swiss Re Says Low Natural-Catastrophe Losses Lift Results
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud


Swiss Re reported a sharp increase in third-quarter net profit and said its results benefited from low natural-catastrophe losses.

Net profit was $1.4 billion for the third quarter and $4 billion for the first nine months as a whole, the Zurich-based reinsurer said Friday. This compares with $102 million and $2.2 billion the company reported for the respective year-earlier periods, when its results took a hit from an increase in U.S. liability reserves to cover future obligations.

Analysts had forecast a quarterly net profit of $1.325 billion, according to consensus estimates provided by the company.

Profit growth was driven by the group's property-and-casualty reinsurance business, for which low natural-catastrophe losses provided a substantial tailwind, Swiss Re said.

A strong performance in the first nine months of the year puts the company on track to meet its target of a net profit of more than $4.4 billion for 2025, Swiss Re Chief Executive Andreas Berger said.

Insurance revenue for the first nine months was $32 billion, down 5% on year. The company posted lower insurance revenue across its property-and-casualty, life-and-health and corporate-solutions segments.


Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-14-25 0142ET

data.title: India new issue - Jio Credit to issue 5-year bonds, bankers say
Japan's Mizuho books 44% rise in second-quarter profit, raises forecast
data.description:
TOKYO (Reuters) -Japan's Mizuho Financial Group on Friday posted a 44% rise in second-quarter net profit.

Profit for July-September reached 399 billion yen ($2.65 billion) compared to 277 billion yen over the same period a year previously, Japan's third-largest banking group said.

Mizuho hiked its profit forecast to 1.13 trillion yen from 1.02 trillion yen for the financial year to March 2026.

($1 = 150.7800 yen)

(Reporting by Anton Bridge; Editing by Christopher Cushing)

data.title: India wholesale prices fall 1.21% year-on-year in October
data.description:
NEW DELHI (Reuters) -India's wholesale prices fell 1.21% year-on-year in October after a 0.13% rise in the previous month, government data showed on Friday.

Economists polled by Reuters had projected the wholesale prices to fall 0.6% year-on-year in October.

(Reporting by Manoj Kumar; Editing by Subhranshu Sahu)

Skanska Sells Office Building in Poland for SEK 680 Million
data.description:
Construction company Skanska has sold the second phase of the Centrum Poludnie office complex in Wroclaw, Poland, for EUR62 million, equivalent to approximately SEK 680 million. The buyer is Investika Real Estate Fund, in partnership with Bud Holdings. This information was disclosed in a press release.

The transaction will be recorded in the fourth quarter of 2025, with immediate transfer of ownership. The phase includes over 21,500 square meters of office and retail space.

data.title: Db Insurance Q3 operating profit 403 billion won, down 33.5% from year earlier
Japan Post Insurance Co Ltd to buy back up to 5.4% of own shares worth 45 billion yen
Orkla Posts Slightly Better Than Expected Results, Announces Multi-Billion Share Buyback
data.description:
Norwegian food conglomerate Orkla has reported a third-quarter earnings report that came in slightly above expectations.

Revenue rose by 4.3 percent to NOK 17,946 million (NOK 17,209 million). The figure beats Bloomberg's analyst consensus, which was NOK 17,750 million.

Operating profit reached NOK 1,638 million (NOK 1,258 million), with an operating margin of 9.1 percent (7.3 percent).

Adjusted operating profit amounted to NOK 2,039 million (NOK 1,998 million), compared to the expected NOK 2,030 million, with an adjusted operating margin of 11.4 percent (11.6 percent).

Profit before tax was NOK 1,997 million (NOK 1,505 million).

Net profit totaled NOK 1,692 million (NOK 1,212 million), compared to the analyst consensus of NOK 1,660 million.

Earnings per share were NOK 1.57 (NOK 1.12).

Adjusted earnings per share reached NOK 1.85 (NOK 1.70), beating the expected NOK 1.70.

Alongside the report, the company announced a share buyback program worth NOK 4 billion. The program will launch on November 17 this year and will be completed no later than December 31, 2026. The buyback is made possible by the stock market listing of Orkla India and the strong cash flow generated by the group.

In the report, CEO Nils Selte praised the portfolio companies for improving adjusted operating profit and margin, as well as return on capital employed, all in line with the financial targets for the current strategy period.

"We remain fully focused on delivering on each of these targets by 2026, with a particular emphasis on continued organic growth and disciplined cost management in the portfolio companies," Selte said.

data.description: Berenberg has lowered its price target for Novo Nordisk to 400 Danish kroner, down from the previous target of 425, while maintaining its buy recommendation, according to BN.

data.title: Mikael Dolsten Withdraws Candidacy for Novo Nordisk Board
data.description:
Danish pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk announced that Mikael Dolsten has withdrawn his candidacy for the board ahead of the extraordinary general meeting scheduled for November 14, citing personal reasons.

The Novo Nordisk Foundation and Novo Holdings have decided not to propose a replacement at this time and instead plan to nominate two additional candidates for the 2026 annual general meeting. All other proposed candidates for the upcoming extraordinary general meeting remain unchanged.

Swedish national Mikael Dolsten most recently served as Chief Scientific Officer at the American company Pfizer.

Cartier-owner Richemont beats quarterly sales forecast as sales jump 14%
data.description:
ZURICH (Reuters) -Cartier-owner Richemont reported better-than-expected quarterly sales on Friday, as the Geneva-based luxury group awaited the outcome of tariff negotiations between Switzerland and the United States.

The U.S. and Switzerland have edged closer to a trade deal to reduce President Donald Trump's crippling 39% tariffs on Swiss imports and shrink Switzerland's trade surplus with the U.S. after talks in Washington on Thursday, officials from the two countries said.

The United States is Richemont's biggest single market, generating around 22% of the company's sales. The company's other brands include watchmakers IWC, Piaget and Jaeger-LeCoultre, as well as jeweller Van Cleef & Arpels.

Quarterly sales were up 14% at constant exchange rates in the July-September period, the company said.

(Reporting by John Revill, editing by Tassilo Hummel)

Google Offers Remedies to EU After $3.4 Billion Ad Tech Fine
data.description:
By Edith Hancock


Alphabet's Google submitted remedies to the European Commission after the bloc's 2.95 billion euros ($3.42 billion) fine over the dominance of the company's advertising technology business, but stopped short of offering to sell parts of the business.

"Our proposal fully addresses the [European Commission's] decision without a disruptive break-up that would harm the thousands of European publishers and advertisers who use Google tools to grow their business," the company said in a statement on Friday.

The company said Friday it submitted a number of proposals to change how its ad tech business works in the EU. They include giving publishers an option to set varied minimum prices for different bidders when using Google's Ad Manager platform, and improving interoperability between Google's tools and products from other businesses to give publishers and advertisers more choice.

Google said it would continue to work with EU officials as they assess the proposal. "We are committed to finding an effective solution that provides certainty and consistency for our customers across Europe, the United States and globally," it said.

The fine issued in September is the EU's second-largest antitrust penalty ever, after an earlier fine issued to Google in 2018. The company has racked up billions in EU competition penalties related to popular services such as its search engine and Android operating system.

EU officials said that Google broke the bloc's competition rules by favoring its own online display ad tech services at the expense of rival providers and online publishers. According to the EU, the company holds an inherent conflict of interest due to its high profile on both the buy and sell-side of the advertising business through services such as Google Ads, Google Ad Manager and its exchange AdX.

The commission asked Google to bring its self-preferencing practices to an end, and to implement measures to end what it sees as its conflicts of interest along the ad tech supply chain.

A spokesperson for the commission confirmed it had received Google's remedy offer. "We will now analyse Google's proposed measures to assess whether they effectively bring the self-preferencing practices to an end and address the situation of inherent conflicts of interest," they said.

Teresa Ribera--the EU's top competition enforcer--told Dow Jones Newswires in September that it's hard to see how the issues identified can be remedied without some kind of structural change, but that Google should have a chance to offer solutions.

The submission also comes as Google faces a parallel legal dispute in the U.S. over its ad tech business. Judges there have yet to rule on a final remedy.

Brussels' enforcement against big technology companies has become a sore point for relations with the U.S. President Donald Trump has criticized EU regulators for fining American tech giants like Google and Apple, likening them to a tax on U.S. companies.


Write to Edith Hancock at edith.hancock@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-14-25 0114ET

data.title: Macquarie Group says Macquarie Asset Management closes second US CLO at $410 million
Allianz posts 15% rise in Q3 net profit after lifting outlook
data.description:
FRANKFURT (Reuters) -Germany's Allianz on Friday posted a better-than-expected 15% rise in third-quarter net profit, figures that came a day after the bank gave a rosier outlook for the full year.

Net profit attributable to shareholders of 2.847 billion euros ($3.32 billion) in the three months through September compares with profit of 2.471 billion euros a year earlier. The figure surpassed a 2.676 billion euro consensus forecast.

($1 = 0.8575 euros)

(Reporting by Tom Sims and Alexander Huebner, Editing by Friederike Heine)
